title: ranunculus arizonicus var. typicus
intro: Ranunculus arizonicus var. typicus, also known as Arizona buttercup, is a perennial wildflower native to the southwestern United States. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Ranunculus arizonicus var. typicus include its ability to reduce inflammation, fight infections, and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and flowers can be used to make teas, infusions, and salves to harness its medicinal properties.
